content:
# Final Review Aggregate

**Summary:** Final review accepted 4 amendment(s); restart required.
**Restart Required:** true
**Force Completed:** false
**Total Reviewers:** 2
**Unique Amendments:** 5
**Final Review Restarts:** 11/20

## Accepted

- `fr-11-de7c4079`
- `fr-11-93ae6a7b`
- `fr-11-3a6e6609`
- `fr-11-3a1b5f20`

## Rejected

- `fr-11-98b466d1`

## Disputed

None.

## Final Accepted Amendments

### `fr-11-de7c4079`

In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs` around lines 1407-1449, the PromptReview resume path does NOT handle `BackendExhausted` during `check_availability` — exhausted validators are treated the same as other errors (fail if required, silently drop if optional). This is inconsistent with the CompletionPanel and FinalReview resume paths (lines 1453-1553) which both skip BackendExhausted members gracefully with quorum reduction. The execution-time probe for PromptReview (line ~6486) DOES handle BackendExhausted correctly, so the inconsistency only affects the resume codepath. A required prompt-review validator that is BackendExhausted during resume will cause a hard ResumeDriftFailure instead of graceful degradation.

### `fr-11-93ae6a7b`

Narrow stderr before calling `is_backend_exhausted` in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1401-1420`, `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1443-1455`, and `src/adapters/tmux.rs:632-643`. Right now `classify_exit_failure_with_output` receives the full stderr, and `is_backend_exhausted` does raw substring matches over that full text in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1768-1774`. Codex stderr includes the echoed user prompt before the final error line (for example `.ralph-burning/projects/9ni-2-4/runtime/failed/run-20260327233800-final_review-reviewer-reviewer-2-c2-a1-cr10.failed.raw:14-186`), so any unrelated non-zero exit will be misclassified as `BackendExhausted` whenever the prompt itself quotes "usage limit", "quota exceeded", etc. Extract or scan only the terminal error portion of stderr, the same way stdout is narrowed, before making the non-retryable classification.

### `fr-11-3a6e6609`

Either remove the new `BackendExhausted` degradation from prompt-review, or finish it end-to-end. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6487-6536` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6607-6618` now reduce prompt-review quorum when validators are exhausted and pass the reduced minimum into execution, but `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:174-195` still propagates any validator invocation error with `?`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:241-247` has no exhausted-member handling at all. A validator that passes availability probing and then exhausts during the actual prompt-review call will still fail the entire stage, so the new engine behavior is inconsistent and only half implemented.

### `fr-11-3a1b5f20`

Preserve probe-time exhaustion information when building completion/final-review aggregates. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6824-6847` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7048-7101` drop exhausted members before execution and compute a reduced minimum; `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6906-6917` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7160-7173` then pass only that reduced minimum into the executors. The aggregate writers in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:279-287`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:534-555`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1415-1496` therefore record `min_completers`/`effective_min_reviewers` and `exhausted_count` using only post-snapshot data, even though `src/contexts/workflow_composition/panel_contracts.rs:116-124` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/panel_contracts.rs:230-236` document those fields as the configured minimum plus exhaustion count. After a probe-time skip, the persisted aggregate can no longer tell that any backend was exhausted, and `CompletionAggregatePayload.min_completers` is no longer the configured value it claims to be.
